name: Insecure Deserialization (Java/PHP/Python Object Injection)
description: Detects indicators of insecure deserialization by identifying serialized object patterns in cookies, hidden form fields, and API responses. Covers Java (rO0AB), PHP (O:, a:), Python (pickle), and .NET (AAEAAAD) serialization formats.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Never deserialize untrusted data. Use JSON or other safe data formats instead of native serialization. Implement integrity checks (HMAC) on serialized objects. Use allowlists for deserialization classes.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: Insecure deserialization leads to remote code execution. Java deserialization (Apache Commons Collections, Log4Shell) has been responsible for some of the largest breaches. Look for rO0AB base64 patterns in logs.
